artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss opportunity to upgrade Wi-Fi access points with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I think we should discuss with the steering committee

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval to add Wi-Fi upgrade to scope
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: get steering committee approval before the March 18 window

extracted_tasks.text: Confirm TechSupplier can include access points in same delivery
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: they can include the access points in the same delivery

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

alerts.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities from Cisco Aironet 2800 series to Catalyst 9120 series during the migration window.
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement...; IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites (specifically switches, not access points).
alerts.suggested_action: Reject the request as a scope change unless formally submitted via Change Control Board (CCB) for approval, as it introduces new hardware procurement ($85k-$110k), timeline impact (+9 days), and work outside the defined baseline.

llm_calls.content:
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming (changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform)",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications",
    "Procurement of new application licenses",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "Project Management Plan (D-001)",
    "Technical Design Document (TDD) (D-002)",
    "Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package (D-003)",
    "Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) (D-004)",
    "User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan (D-005)",
    "Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) (D-006)",
    "Post-Migration Validation Report (D-007)",
    "Project Closure Report (D-008)"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes",
    "Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission",
    "Go-Live deadline: April 30, 2026",
    "Project closure deadline: June 30, 2026",
    "Extensions beyond deadlines require ADM-level approval",
    "Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management",
    "Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables",
    "Scope changes require written approval from Project Sponsor and impact assessment"
  ]
}

Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)
Application modernization or re-platforming (changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform)
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)
End-user training and change management beyond project communications
constraints: Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes
Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission
Extensions beyond deadlines require ADM-level approval
Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management
Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables
Scope changes require written approval from Project Sponsor and impact assessment

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.25,
  "drift_type": "new_requirement",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities from Cisco Aironet 2800 series to Catalyst 9120 series during the migration window.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement...; IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites (specifically switches, not access points).",
  "suggested_action": "Reject the request as a scope change unless formally submitted via Change Control Board (CCB) for approval, as it introduces new hardware procurement ($85k-$110k), timeline impact (+9 days), and work outside the defined baseline.",
  "reasoning": "The document requests the replacement of Wireless Access Points (WAPs), which is not listed in the 'IN SCOPE' items (which only cover Catalyst 9500 switches) nor explicitly excluded as a minor task. While it falls under 'network infrastructure,' the baseline specifically defines the scope as switch deployment and server migration. Adding WAP replacement constitutes a new hardware procurement and installation requirement that impacts both budget (exceeding current estimates) and schedule, requiring formal change management."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities from Cisco Aironet 2800 series to Catalyst 9120 series during the migration window.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement...; IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites (specifically switches, not access points).
parsed_json.suggested_action: Reject the request as a scope change unless formally submitted via Change Control Board (CCB) for approval, as it introduces new hardware procurement ($85k-$110k), timeline impact (+9 days), and work outside the defined baseline.
parsed_json.reasoning: The document requests the replacement of Wireless Access Points (WAPs), which is not listed in the 'IN SCOPE' items (which only cover Catalyst 9500 switches) nor explicitly excluded as a minor task. While it falls under 'network infrastructure,' the baseline specifically defines the scope as switch deployment and server migration. Adding WAP replacement constitutes a new hardware procurement and installation requirement that impacts both budget (exceeding current estimates) and schedule, requiring formal change management.
